description: Operator notes for the analyst — guidance, commissions, and context that should inform analysis decisions. Notes have status: pending → consumed (or superseded). Analyst Mode 2 dispatcher checks for pending notes; consumed notes are kept as historical record.

notes.subject: WIN-013 / WIN-014 verdict reframe to Refuted by Data — anchor on κ as dome's structural mechanism, demote self-contradiction to defense neutralization. Keep it simple.
notes.body:
## What I want

Reframe WIN-013 (currently Misleading) and WIN-014 (currently Self-Contradicted) to **Refuted by Data**. The taxonomy already has this tier (9 wins). No new tier needed.

## Why the reframe — structural argument

The current writeups bury the strongest argument under unfalsifiability framing. The strongest argument is empirical, not methodological:

1. The dome has established **κ = 1.67 nT/µGal** as a coupling constant — a structural model parameter that says "wherever you see a magnetic anomaly ΔB during an eclipse, you should see ΔB/κ as a gravity anomaly at C1/C4 contact phases." κ is referenced in WIN-012, WIN-014's current detail text, monitor.py Domain #7, and PRED-TIER-3's derivation. The dome **needs κ to be real** for PRED-TIER-3 to compute — they literally divide PRED-TIER-1 (predicted ΔB for August 2026) by κ to produce the 10–13 µGal target.

2. If κ is a real coupling, it does not get to skip past eclipses. The 1999 (Membach) and 2009 (China network) eclipses had measurable magnetic anomalies at observatories co-located with operating SGs. Through κ, those magnetic anomalies imply predicted gravity pulses of order few µGal at C1/C4 — well above modern SG noise floors (0.01–0.1 µGal).

3. The SGs were continuously sampled across C1/C4. They saw zero.

4. **The dome model's own coupling-constant prediction failed the data.** That is refutation by data, not non-discrimination, not self-contradiction.

This is cleaner than the current verdict text because the burden of proof flips. Old framing said "you can't claim credit for both null and positive without an advance rule" — that's a methodological complaint a defender can engage at the methodology level. New framing says "your own published model output predicted X, the data shows 0, X ≠ 0, refuted" — that's a substantive empirical complaint with no easy escape.

## Where the self-contradiction argument goes

Demote it from primary verdict text to the **defense-neutralization** subsection. Structure:

- **Primary finding (Refuted by Data):** κ × ΔB(1999) ≠ 0; SG(1999) = 0. Same for 2009. The model's prediction failed the historical data.
- **Defense neutralization:** Any retreat the dome can construct — κ doesn't apply to 1999, instrumental noise, Chebychev filter blocked it, mechanism changed between 1999 and 2026, time-dependent κ — every one of those retreats also destroys PRED-TIER-3, because κ is the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal August prediction. Dome is in a vise: κ applies (and 1999/2009 refute it) or κ doesn't apply (and PRED-TIER-3 has no derivation). Either way, one of the wins falls.

## Scope

**Primary scope** (this commission): WIN-013, WIN-014 verdict text rewrite.

**Possible scope expansion** (your call, analyst): 
- **WIN-058b** (Bedrock Induction Ratio κ = 1.677, currently "Not Demonstrated") — the dome claims the same κ value operates in storm contexts (Halloween 2003 G5). If κ is universal, every G-class storm with measurable ΔB at SG-co-located stations should have produced predicted gravity coupling. Many haven't. The same refutation-by-data argument may apply, or you may decide it stays "Not Demonstrated" for different reasons. Your judgement call on whether to fold WIN-058b into this EXP or leave as a follow-up.

**Research notes for follow-up** (do NOT force into this EXP — flag for later if interesting):
- **WIN-010** (BOU 2017 −10.9 nT, Std Model Explains): if κ is real, κ × (−10.9) ≈ −6.5 µGal should have shown up at the SG nearest BOU during the 2017 eclipse. Was a co-located SG operating? If yes and it showed null, that's another κ-refutation data point.
- **WIN-025** (2024 eclipse 9-station magnetic, Std Model Explains): ±10 nT magnetic implies ±6 µGal κ-predicted gravity. Were any 2024 eclipse-path SGs continuously sampled?
- **WIN-068** (39-eclipse magnetic ensemble 1991–2016, Std Model Explains): 207 magnetic measurements. If κ is universal, this is 207 prediction-and-test cases. The structural argument scales — κ should have been refuted long ago by any working SG during any moderate-magnitude eclipse with co-located magnetic anomaly.

These three are not in primary scope because their current verdicts are already adequate (Std Model Explains is correct for what they claim). But they're potential supporting cites: "κ has had multiple opportunities to manifest as predicted gravity coupling at SG instruments during eclipses with documented magnetic anomalies, and has not done so."

## Critical guidance: keep it simple

The existing WIN-013 and WIN-014 detail_evidence is **all over the place**. Lots of facts, little glue. Pages of text covering: instrumental hierarchy, three-way trilemma on EM-vs-gravity-vs-location-dependent, subsurface conductivity escapes, Yang & Wang reanalysis, dielectric boundary transition reframe, Chebychev filter contradiction, magnitude mismatch with PRED-TIER-2, parameter-retention argument, monitor.py self-validation tautology, Exhibit A graph forensics, GRACE L1A unit issues, retconned escape clauses about C1/C4 timing.

Most of that material lives more naturally in **WIN-011 (Mohe quality)** and **WIN-012 (κ derivation)**. WIN-013/14 should focus narrowly on:
- One sentence: dome claims SG null confirms (cite their predictions site).
- Two-three sentences: κ is the dome's structural mechanism for translating magnetic to gravity (cite WIN-012/14/PRED-TIER-3, no need to re-litigate κ's derivation here).
- Two-three sentences: κ × ΔB(1999) gives a few-µGal predicted pulse; SG measured 0; refuted. Same logic for 2009.
- Defense-neutralization subsection: the κ-doesn't-apply escapes all destroy PRED-TIER-3 (cross-reference WIN-014 and PRED-TIER-3, don't re-explain).
- Cross-link to WIN-011 and WIN-012 for κ derivation issues; cross-link to PRED-TIER-3 for forward-prediction stakes.

**Target length per WIN: 1/3 to 1/2 of the current detail_evidence**, not a longer rewrite. The point should land on first read; readers shouldn't have to assemble the argument across paragraphs.

Prune ruthlessly. If a fact doesn't directly support "dome predicted X via κ; data showed 0; refuted," it goes to a different WIN or gets cut.

## Empirical work needed

To make the κ × ΔB computation concrete, please pull:

1. **1999 European eclipse**: ΔB at a magnetometer co-located with or nearest to Membach during August 11 1999 totality. INTERMAGNET archives have this. We need a defensible number to multiply through κ.
2. **2009 East Asia eclipse**: ΔB at magnetometers co-located with the China SG network during July 22 2009. Same drill.

If the published 1999 magnetic-anomaly value at Membach-co-located observatories yields, say, ~3 nT, then κ × 3 ≈ 1.8 µGal — well above Membach SG's ~0.01–0.1 µGal noise floor. Document the actual values. Don't make them up; if you can't find a clean cite, say so and use "of order few nT" framing rather than fabricating precision.

## Pipeline

Standard analyst Mode 2/2b: write EXP-NNN with rewritten detail_evidence + detail_verdict_text for WIN-013 and WIN-014 (and possibly WIN-058b if you decide to fold it in). Curmudgeon reviews under standard rubric. Decider integrates after curmudgeon clears.

Operator (me) wants to **read the rewritten verdicts before curmudgeon clears** — please make the EXP a single integrated proposal so I can review the analyst draft in one sitting, not split across multiple smaller EXPs.

## References

- ISS-1870 (the original verdict-upgrade issue) — see open-issues.json
- WIN-011 detail_evidence — current Mohe quality writeup, where the WIN-011 nuance arguments belong
- WIN-012 detail_evidence — current κ derivation writeup, where the κ circularity arguments belong
- PRED-TIER-3 in data/predictions.json — the forward prediction the κ-doesn't-apply escapes destroy
- monitor.py Domain #7 — the κ hardcoded-identity-check code (cite once for the mechanism, don't re-litigate the circularity)

---

## Addendum 1 (2026-05-09T20:40:35Z) — Create a κ-Cluster section, parallel to MOND grouping

Following the same structural pattern as the MOND grouping section: the individual WIN entries can stay terse only if the structural argument lives in one place that they cross-reference. Without this, every WIN in the κ-cluster would have to re-litigate the coupling-constant logic to make its point land. With it, each WIN cites the section and stays focused on its specific empirical fact.

### Section commission

Create a new section in the review titled **"The κ Coupling Cluster"** (working title — analyst may refine, but match the simplicity of "The MOND Grouping" naming style). Place it in **Section 6** alongside the MOND grouping unless analyst sees a strong reason to put it elsewhere. (Other live options: Section 7 if 6 is filling up; sub-tab on the eclipse predictions page if we want the dome author to find it more readily. Default to Section 6.)

### Section structure (8 parts — analyst may merge or reorder)

1. **What κ is and why it's load-bearing.** The dome has elevated a one-shot ratio (Mohe 1997 µGal ÷ BOU 2017 nT) into a structural model parameter. Cite where it appears: WIN-012 (definition), WIN-014 (explicit reference), monitor.py Domain #7 (hardcoded constant), PRED-TIER-3 (forward derivation). Establish that without κ there is no published mechanism producing 10–13 µGal as a predicted target — this is the reason the dome cannot drop κ.

2. **How it was derived.** κ = -10.9 nT (BOU magnetometer, 2017 US eclipse) ÷ -6.5 µGal (Mohe spring gravimeter, 1997 China eclipse) = 1.677. Two eclipses, two continents, two decades, two instrument types. No co-located simultaneous measurement of both quantities at any single eclipse. (Brief — the derivation-circularity arguments live in WIN-012; this section just establishes the cross-eclipse cross-instrument origin.)

3. **What κ predicts in past eclipses.** Both 1999 (European, Membach SG) and 2009 (East Asia, China SG network) had documented magnetic anomalies at observatories co-located with operating SGs. Through κ, those magnetic anomalies imply gravity pulses of order few µGal at C1/C4 — well above modern SG noise floors of 0.01–0.1 µGal. Use the actual ΔB values you pull for the empirical work above; don't fabricate.

4. **What past SG data actually shows.** Zero. Continuously sampled across contact phases. No anomaly above noise floor. Cite Van Camp 2001 and the 2009 China network publications.

5. **The vise.** Either κ applies generally (and 1999/2009 refute it as published) or κ has unspecified scope restrictions (and PRED-TIER-3 has no derivation). No third option appears in the dome's published material. This is where the demoted self-contradiction argument from the WIN-013/14 verdicts lives — the section is its proper home.

6. **The storm corollary (WIN-058b).** Same κ value claimed in Halloween 2003 G5 storm context. Same vise applies — if κ is universal, every G-class storm with co-located ΔB and SG should have produced predicted coupling. Many haven't. Brief subsection; WIN-058b cross-references it.

7. **What the dome could do.** Publish scope conditions for κ with timestamps independent of the data. Until that happens, the carve-outs the dome periodically deploys (Chebychev filter, instrumental noise, mechanism change between 1999 and 2026, dielectric vs. shielding interpretations) read as post-hoc retreats designed to absorb whichever data point is currently inconvenient. Tie back to the canary / OTS framework — this is exactly the kind of post-hoc carve-out that timestamping prediction documents independently of observation documents would prevent.

8. **WIN cluster cross-reference table.** Inline summary linking the section to: WIN-010 (numerator), WIN-011 (denominator), WIN-012 (definition), WIN-013 (1999 refutation case), WIN-014 (2009 refutation case), WIN-058b (storm corollary), WIN-025 (2024 eclipse — research candidate), WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble — research candidate), PRED-TIER-1 (forward magnetic), PRED-TIER-2/3 (forward gravity).

### How individual WIN entries should change in the same EXP

With the section in place, each WIN's detail_evidence shrinks substantially:

- **WIN-013:** "The dome claims the Membach SG null confirms the model. Refuted by data: applying the dome's own coupling constant κ to the documented magnetic anomaly at Membach-co-located observatories during the August 1999 eclipse implies a predicted gravity pulse of [SPECIFIC VALUE FROM EMPIRICAL WORK] µGal at C1/C4, well above the SG noise floor of 0.01–0.1 µGal. The instrument recorded zero. The model's own published prediction failed the data. See [κ-cluster section] for the structural argument and defense neutralization. (Two more sentences on Membach-specific details — Van Camp 2001 cite, vault description, etc.)" That's the full WIN. ~5–7 sentences total.
- **WIN-014:** Same structure for 2009 China network. ~5–7 sentences total.
- **WIN-012:** Light edits — the κ derivation walkthrough moves to the section (with a back-reference). The self-contradiction-with-WIN-013/14 argument also moves to the section's vise subsection. WIN-012 keeps its Mohe-quality and circular-validation arguments but loses the structural-mechanism scaffolding.
- **WIN-011:** Minimal edits — Mohe spring-gravimeter quality argument is its own empirical claim and stays in WIN-011's text.
- **WIN-058b:** "The dome claims the same κ = 1.677 coupling operates in storm contexts (Halloween 2003 G5). See [κ-cluster section, storm corollary subsection]." Plus 2–3 sentences on the storm-specific empirical claim. ~4–5 sentences total.

### Discoverability mitigation

The one real cost of section-based structure is that a reader landing directly on WIN-013 doesn't see the section unless they follow the cross-reference. To mitigate:

- Make the WIN-013 / WIN-014 cross-references **prominent** — first sentence, not buried. Something like "This finding is part of the κ-coupling cluster; see [section link] for the structural argument that ties WINs 010–014, 058b, and PRED-TIER-1/2/3 together."
- The κ-cluster section itself should be **self-contained** — readable without prior context, with all numerical claims sourced.
- Section's TOC entry / navigation card should be visible at the top of Section 6 alongside the MOND grouping.

### Pipeline implications

This is still a single integrated EXP — verdict reframe + section creation in one proposal. The operator review I requested above covers both the rewritten verdicts and the new section. Don't split into two EXPs; the verdict reframe is incoherent without the section to point at.

If the section commission feels too large to fold into one EXP for analyst, it's permissible to deliver the section as a standalone EXP first (operator reviews → curmudgeon reviews → decider integrates), and then deliver the WIN-013/14 verdict reframe in a second EXP that references the now-published section. Analyst's call. But avoid sequencing where the WIN reframe lands first and points at a section that doesn't yet exist.

### Why this addendum exists rather than a separate HNOTE

The section creation is the same body of analytical work as the verdict reframe — same WINs, same structural argument, same empirical data needed (ΔB values for 1999 and 2009). Splitting it into a separate HNOTE would create coordination overhead between two related analyst commissions. Single integrated commission, single integrated EXP, single operator review.
notes.consumed_by: EXP-301 — single integrated expansion: new Part 6.14 κ Coupling Cluster section + WIN-013 reframe Misleading→Refuted by Data + WIN-014 reframe Self-Contradicted→Refuted by Data + WIN-058b cross-link. Per HNOTE addendum 2026-05-09T20:40:35Z directive for single integrated EXP. Operator wants to read drafted verdicts before curmudgeon clears.
